perturbation.original_text: 1.2 Approved Location. The street address of the location for the Franchised Business approved under this Agreement is specified in Exhibit A to this Agreement, and is referred to as the "Approved Location."
perturbation.changed_text: 1.2 Approved Location. The location for the Franchised Business under this Agreement is referred to as the "Approved Location."
perturbation.explanation: The perturbation removes the clause specifying that the street address is found in Exhibit A. This creates an in-text contradiction if subsequent sections refer to a specific street address for the Approved Location by referencing Exhibit A. For example, Section 5.4, 'Lease Review,' mandates providing a copy of the proposed lease for the 'Approved Location,' creating uncertainty about how this location is definitively identified without a clearly defined address.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5.4 Lease Review. You agree to provide us with a copy of the proposed lease, sublease, or purchase agreement for the Approved Location, and you agree not to enter into that lease, sublease, or purchase agreement until you have received our written approval. We have the right to condition our approval of the lease, sublease, or purchase agreement upon the inclusion of terms that we find acceptable and that are consistent with our rights and your responsibilities under this Agreement, including without limitation, that you and the landlord execute a Lease Rider in the form attached to this Agreement as Exhibit H. You also agree to provide us with a copy of the fully signed lease, and lease rider before you begin construction or renovations as the Approved Location.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the explicit link to Exhibit A for the Approved Location's address. Section 5.4 then refers to the 'Approved Location' for lease review, implying a specific, identifiable location. By removing the reference to Exhibit A, the contract now lacks a clear method for definitively determining the Approved Location's street address. This creates a contradiction because Section 5.4 assumes the Approved Location is easily identifiable, which it no longer is.

perturbation.original_text: 4.2 Royalty Fee. We will receive all Commissions (defined below) from insurance carriers. We will receive all Premiums (defined below), Policy Fees (defined below) and Agency Fees on your behalf. If the event that any Premiums, Policy Fees, or Agency Fees are received directly by you, these funds must be forwarded to us within twenty four (24) hours of receipt. We will retain Agency Fees and will forward Premiums and Policy Fees to the insurance carriers. Beginning on the date you begin operations under this Agreement, which is agreed to be the date set out in the Declarations Page (the "Commencement Date"), we will remit to you Net Revenues on a monthly basis. As used in this Agreement:
perturbation.changed_text: 4.2 Royalty Fee. We will receive all Commissions (defined below) from insurance carriers. We will receive all Premiums, Policy Fees and Agency Fees on your behalf. If the event that any Premiums, Policy Fees, or Agency Fees are received directly by you, these funds must be forwarded to us within twenty four (24) hours of receipt. We will retain Agency Fees and will forward Premiums and Policy Fees to the insurance carriers. Beginning on the date you begin operations under this Agreement, we will remit to you Net Revenues on a monthly basis. As used in this Agreement:
perturbation.explanation: The perturbation removes the reference to "(defined below)" after 'Premiums', 'Policy Fees', and 'Agency Fees'. This generates an in-text contradiction because while the contract mentions these fees, their specific definitions are no longer directly indicated in this section. This contradiction particularly impacts Section 4.3, 'Monthly Accounting,' which references these fees, but without clear in-text definitions, uncertainty arises regarding how those fees are supposed to be calculated and what they entails to make enforcement ambiguous, as those terms are never defined within the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4.3 Monthly Accounting. Once a Month, the insurance carriers will send a commission report and Commissions earned by you, to us. On the 25th day of each Month, unless this Agreement has been terminated for any reason, we will pay to you the Net Revenues for all policies identified in a commission detail report that we receive from the insurance carrier. Please note that we expect each insurance carrier to submit commission detail reports on a Monthly basis for all policies written during the preceding Month by no later than the 20th day of the subsequent Month. But, if a carrier does not provide us with a commission detail report (and the applicable Commission) by the 20th day of the Month, or if a policy is not identified in the commission detail report we receive, you will not receive the Gross Revenues for those policies until the insurance carrier provides us with the appropriate report and/or Commissions. We may delay or withhold payment of Net Revenues — on a policy by policy basis — for any policy for which you fail to observe the risk management procedures we prescribe in the Manual, including that you obtain a signed application from the customer and provide all required documentation. If we review your accounting and client records (as described in Section 12 below) and find that you have not forwarded to us any Premiums, Policy Fees and/or Agency Fees that you collect, we may pay the appropriate Premiums and Policy Fees to the insurance carrier. You will be responsible for reimbursing us for those amounts and the applicable Agency Fees, in addition to paying a fee to us to cover our reasonable expenses in processing those payments and interest on those amounts, at the rate of two percent (2%) per Month, or if less, the maximum rate permitted by law. Entitlement to such interest will be in addition to any other remedies we may have.
perturbation.justification: The changed text omits the phrase '(defined below)' after 'Premiums', 'Policy Fees', and 'Agency Fees' in Section 4.2. This creates a direct contradiction with Section 4.3, which references these same fees. By removing the indication that these terms are explicitly defined within the contract, it introduces ambiguity and uncertainty regarding their calculation and scope, making the enforcement of obligations regarding these fees in Section 4.3 ambiguous. This aligns with the definition of 'Omissions - In Text Contradiction' because the removal creates a conflict regarding obligations in another section.

perturbation.original_text: 6.3.3 Your Operating Principal, and all of your trainees, Managers, and Producers must sign and deliver to us a personal covenant of confidentiality, an in-term non-competition agreement, and a post-term non-competition agreement in substantially the form of Exhibit F to this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 6.3.3 Your Operating Principal, and all of your trainees, Managers, and Producers must sign and deliver to us a personal covenant of confidentiality, an in-term non-competition agreement, and a post-term non-competition agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The perturbation removes the phrase "in substantially the form of Exhibit F to this Agreement." This creates an in-text contradiction because, while the section states these agreements must be signed, it no longer specifies the required form via Exhibit F. This lack of specification clashes with Section 8.10.4, which requires guarantees 'in the form attached as Exhibit B,' thereby creating ambiguity about the enforceability and required format for the confidentiality and non-competition agreements. The omitted clause makes those agreements uncertain due to the lack of explicit reference to the format.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8.10.4 Guarantees. You agree to obtain, and deliver to us, a guarantee of your performance under this Agreement and covenant concerning confidentiality and competition, in the form attached as Exhibit B, from each current and future direct and indirect: (a) shareholder of a corporate Franchisee; (b) member of a limited liability company Franchisee; (c) partner of a partnership Franchisee; and/or (d) partner of a limited liability partnership Franchisee.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 6.3.3 removes the requirement for the confidentiality and non-competition agreements to be 'in substantially the form of Exhibit F'. This omission directly contradicts Section 8.10.4, which mandates that guarantees of performance and covenants concerning confidentiality and competition be 'in the form attached as Exhibit B'. Without a specified form in 6.3.3, it becomes unclear whether Exhibit B is the correct form for the confidentiality and non-competition agreements, creating a direct conflict and ambiguity regarding the required format and enforceability of these agreements.
